channel.title: Comments for page "I Stand Atop a Spiral Stair"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "I Stand Atop a Spiral Stair"

item.title: home to CH A N G E
item.content:encoded:
“the board had been set and I was playing white.”
I love that!
I’m quite unfamiliar with lore/tales on the site, but I was snooping around and my brain just said “IT’S RUSH LYRICS YOU GOTTA CLICK IT!” I think the strongest ‘grabbers’ for skips and such are the titles themselves, as it’s the only thing you can conclude about the entry without reading it. And I’m a sucker for obscure references, so this was irresistible.
It was a bit hard to understand the setting at first, but it got clearer throughout the story. It’s a bit frustrating in the beginning, but I love this technique because you get that feeling of being smacked in the face with knowledge when it all comes together. Then, you can go over and read it again with a deeper appreciation! Nice!
(It may be possible that everything would be obvious if I actually knew the lore, but I got it anyway so it’s all good)
I will definitely be recommending this to a friend!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hey you should go read that second tale
I hear a nerd wrote it
go make fun of him

You're a nerd and this is a nerd tale.

how dare you make me have emotions take your upvote you monster

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolute emotional frisson right there. Cross-linking manages to paint shades of a wider world without being overtly intrusive. The feels here also kind of touch a nerve I can't quite put my finger on. It's chillingly intimate. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This tale is wonderful.

As I've said in chat and I'll say again here, this is a really new direction for the Anderson Robotics skips, and the GoI as a whole, and your shout out to all my characters is delightful.

I'm glad you posted this.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had just finished reading this in your sandbox when you told me in chat that you'd posted it. I adore this; it's vivid and emotional and intelligently written. And thanks for the shoutout, let's coldpost tales into the sunset together

item.title: dedications
item.content:encoded:
He leads me on lightyears away!
Through astral nights, galactic days…
— Rush, 2112

Dedicated to Jacob Conwell, without whom this would not have been possible, and TyGently, for being both an inspiration throughout the writing of this and without whom this would not have been possible. Redundancy gets a shoutout for acknowledgement of redundancy. FFTransform gets credit for the character of the other space lesbian Alexandra Poole, as does the rest of the SCP RP group for also reading partial drafts of this tale while it was still a foetus.

This is another one of my Pure Feels Tales(tm) alongside A Memorandum. As such, expect some sloppiness and coldpost a la Cyantreuse.

Happy New Belated Christmas Years' Eve.

The theme song for this tale is Jamie xx's Loud Places, because 2112 sounded far too happy.
